Yüksel COŞKUNYÜREK ON BEHALF OF THE AK PARTY GROUP (Bolu) - Mr. President, dear deputies; I respectfully salute your noble delegation and our beloved nation.
As you know, energy is indispensable in all of our lives as well as for the country's economy.
It is a lifeblood. I would like to proudly state that in twenty-two years, with the AK Party government, there has been a revolution in the field of energy. Factors such as young and dynamic population structure, developing industrial capacity, increasing urbanization and changing consumption habits increase Türkiye's energy demand day by day. In order to meet this increasing energy demand without any problems, our electrical energy installed power has been quadrupled from 30 thousand megawatts to 119 thousand megawatts by the end of May 2025. Domestic and renewable energy resources are the basis of this increase, and 61 percent of our installed power consists of renewable resources. Our solar and wind energy installed capacity has exceeded 36 thousand megawatts. Our country ranks 5th in Europe and 11th in the world with its total renewable energy installed capacity. We aim for our country's wind and solar energy installed power to reach 120 thousand megawatts by 2035.
Dear MPs, as a result of the steps taken and investments made in the natural gas sector in the last twenty-two years, natural gas is available in 5 provinces and 57 settlements in 2002, and as of 2025, natural gas is used in 81 provinces and 920 settlements. Natural gas service is planned for approximately 100 settlements in 2025 and 2026. Our natural gas network length has been increased from 16 thousand kilometers to 245 thousand kilometers as of today. In parallel with the expanding natural gas supply, our natural gas consumption has reached 53 billion cubic meters.
Our population, which has natural gas access, has exceeded 22 million with the number of subscribers, and has exceeded 72 million; This shows that 85 percent of our country has the opportunity to use natural gas.
We continue to provide support to ensure that our citizens are not affected by high global energy costs. In this context, 56 percent of low-level electricity bills, 34 percent of high-level electricity bills, and 44 percent of natural gas bills continue to be covered by our state. Per unit price for electricity in residential buildings. (Noise from CHP benches)
ALIYE TEAM ERSEVER (Ankara) - It also comes from us.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Here, complete it.
YÜKSEL COŞKUNYÜREK (Continued) - Look, listen to this: We are the cheapest country in Europe per unit price for residential electricity and the cheapest country after Ukraine for natural gas. In addition, the share of 200 kilowatt electricity consumption in residential dwellings in the minimum wage was 20 percent in 2002, while today it is 2.3 percent; While its share in the lowest pension was 15 percent in 2002, it is 3 percent today. While the share of 100 cubic meters of natural gas consumption in residential buildings in the minimum wage was 25 percent in 2002, today it is 5.7 percent; The lowest pension rate was 19.5 percent, but today it is 7.5 percent. We will continue the support we provide to our citizens. "Natural gas has been increased.

records.originalTurkish:
AK PARTİ GRUBU ADINA YÜKSEL COŞKUNYÜREK (Bolu) - Sayın Başkan, çok değerli milletvekilleri; DEM PARTİ Grubunun vermiş olduğu öneri üzerine AK PARTİ Grubumuz adına söz almış bulunmaktayım. Yüce heyetinizi ve aziz milletimizi
saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Bugün burada, ülkemizin can damarı olan, gece gündüz demeden direksiyon sallayarak hanesine helal rızık götüren, aynı zamanda, ekonomimizin çarklarını döndüren nakliyeci, kamyoncu ve tır şoförü esnafımızın meselelerini konuşmak üzere huzurunuzdayım. Konuşmama başlarken şunu net bir şekilde ifade etmek isterim ki biz "İnsanı yaşat ki devlet yaşasın." düsturunu benimseyerek bütün çalışmalarımızda esnafımızın alın terini dikkate alarak onlara en iyi, en kaliteli hizmeti sunmak için gece gündüz çalışıyoruz. Özellikle son yirmi üç yılda baktığımızda, Türkiye'nin ulaşım ve lojistik altyapısında gerçekleştirdiğimiz devrim niteliğindeki adımlar taşımacı esnafımıza verdiğimiz değerin en somut örneğidir. Değerli milletvekilleri, hükûmetlerimiz döneminde ulaşım alanında tam 300 milyar dolarlık devasa bir yatırım gerçekleştirdik. Bu rakam sadece bir istatistik değil aşılmaz denilen dağların tünellerle delinmesi, geçilmez denilen boğazların köprülerle bağlanmasıdır. Son yirmi üç yılda bölünmüş yol uzunluğumuz 6 bin kilometreden 30 bin kilometreye çıktı, otoyol ağımızı 1.700 kilometreden 3.700 kilometreye çıkardık ve modernize ederek nakliyeci esnafımızın hem vaktinden hem de aracının ömründen tasarruf etmesini sağladık. Yollarımızı sadece asfalt yığınları olarak görmedik, her bir yolu esnafımızın güvenle seyahat edeceği birer huzur koridoru hâline getirdik. Yollarımızı akıllı ulaşım sistemleriyle donatıyoruz, trafik yoğunluğunu düşürürken kaza risklerini de bu sayede azaltıyoruz. 2003 yılında 4 bin olan taşımacı esnaf sayımız bugün itibarıyla 682 bine, ticari taşıt filomuz da 1,8 milyona ulaşmıştır.
Değerli milletvekilleri, biliyoruz ki nakliye sektörünün en büyük maliyeti akaryakıttır. Bugün dünyada, özellikle
Orta Doğu'da yaşanan sıcak çatışmalar ve Hürmüz Boğazı gibi stratejik noktalarda yaşanan gerilimler küresel enerji piyasalarında ciddi dalgalanmalara ve arz güvenliği sorunlarına yol açmaktadır. Petrol fiyatlarının dünya genelinde tırmandığı bu zorlu konjonktürde biz de esnafımızı asla kaderine terk etmedik. Küresel fiyat artışlarının nakliyeci esnafımızın dolayısıyla vatandaşımızın sofrasına yansımaması için kararlı bir duruş sağladık ve eşelmobil sistemini getirerek vergi düzenlemesiyle fiyat artışlarını göğüsledik, esnafımızın sırtındaki maliyet yükünü hafifletmek için devlet olarak fedakârlıktan kaçınmadık. Bizim derdimiz şoför kardeşimizin deposunu doldururken yarınını düşünmeden yola çıkabilmesidir.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Tamamlayın lütfen.
YÜKSEL COŞKUNYÜREK (Devamla) - Sadece Türkiye'de değil tüm dünyada akaryakıt fiyatlarında ciddi bir artış yaşanırken bunun piyasa şartlarında en az artışını sağlayan ülkelerden biri Türkiye oldu. Ayrıca, çoğu ülkede akaryakıtta arz sorunu yaşanırken, yakıta erişimde sıkıntı yaşanırken Hükûmetimizin aldığı tedbirlerle ülkemizde bu sorunlar yaşanmıyor.
Biz esnafımızla her zaman omuz omuza olduk. Salgın döneminde, küresel krizlerde ve bölgesel savaşların gölgesinde her zaman "Esnaf varsa devlet var." dedik. Hükûmet olarak bugüne kadar olduğu gibi bundan sonra da ticaretin can damarı olan taşımacı esnafımızın en büyük destekçisi olmaya devam edeceğiz.
Cumhurbaşkanımız Sayın Recep Tayyip Erdoğan'ın liderliğinde, yirmi dört yılda ulaştırmanın her alanında ortaya konulan başarı Türkiye Yüzyılı hedefleri doğrultusunda artarak devam edecektir.
Bu duygu ve düşüncelerle Gazi Meclisi ve siz değerli milletvekillerini saygıyla selamlıyorum. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
Yüksel COŞKUNYÜREK ON BEHALF OF THE AK PARTY GROUP (Bolu) - Mr. President, dear deputies; I have taken the floor on behalf of our AK PARTY Group upon the suggestion made by the DEM PARTY Group. Your noble delegation and our beloved nation
I salute you with respect.
Today, I am here before you to talk about the issues of our tradesmen, who are transporters, truckers and truck drivers, who are the lifeblood of our country, who bring halal sustenance to their households by steering day and night, and who also turn the wheels of our economy. As I begin my speech, I would like to express this clearly that we are "Keeping people alive so that the state can live." By adopting the motto, we work day and night to provide them with the best, highest quality service, taking into account the sweat of our tradesmen in all our work. Especially when we look at the last twenty-three years, the revolutionary steps we have taken in Türkiye's transportation and logistics infrastructure are the most concrete example of the value we give to our transport tradesmen. Dear MPs, we made a huge investment of 300 billion dollars in the field of transportation during our government's term. This figure is not just a statistic; it is the drilling of so-called impenetrable mountains with tunnels and the connection of so-called impassable straits with bridges. In the last twenty-three years, our divided road length has increased from 6 thousand kilometers to 30 thousand kilometers, we have increased our highway network from 1,700 kilometers to 3,700 kilometers, and by modernizing it, we have enabled our transporters to save both time and the life of their vehicles. We did not see our roads just as asphalt piles, we turned each road into a corridor of peace where our tradesmen can travel safely. We equip our roads with smart transportation systems, reducing traffic density and thus reducing the risk of accidents. The number of transport tradesmen, which was 4 thousand in 2003, has reached 682 thousand as of today, and our commercial vehicle fleet has reached 1.8 million.
Dear MPs, we know that the biggest cost of the transportation industry is fuel. In the world today, especially
Hot conflicts in the Middle East and tensions at strategic points such as the Strait of Hormuz cause serious fluctuations in global energy markets and supply security problems. In this difficult conjuncture where oil prices were climbing worldwide, we never abandoned our tradesmen to their fate. We took a determined stance to prevent global price increases from being reflected on the tables of our transport tradesmen and therefore our citizens, and we faced the price increases with tax regulation by introducing the Eselmobil system, and as a state, we did not hesitate to make sacrifices to alleviate the cost burden on our tradesmen. Our concern is that our driver brother can hit the road without thinking about the future while filling his tank.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Complete it, please.
YÜKSEL COŞKUNYÜREK (Continued) - While there was a serious increase in fuel prices not only in Türkiye but all over the world, Türkiye was one of the countries that achieved the least increase in this rate under market conditions. In addition, while most countries experience fuel supply problems and access to fuel, these problems do not occur in our country with the measures taken by our Government.
We have always been shoulder to shoulder with our tradesmen.

During the epidemic, in global crises and in the shadow of regional wars, it is always said: "If there are tradesmen, there is the state." we said. As the government, we will continue to be the biggest supporter of our transport tradesmen, who are the lifeblood of trade, as we have done so far.
Under the leadership of our President, Mr. Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, the success achieved in all areas of transportation in twenty-four years will continue increasingly in line with the goals of the Türkiye Century.
With these feelings and thoughts, I respectfully greet the Gazi Assembly and you, our valued members of parliament. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)
